sections.title: Nominative Case Review
sections.explanation: The nominative case is the basic dictionary form. It's used for the subject — who or what does the action. It answers хто? (who?) or що? (what?).
sections.tip: Nominative = the doer. No ending changes needed!

exercises.prompt: Which word is in the nominative case? 'Хлопець читає книгу.'
exercises.explanation: 'Хлопець' (the boy) is the subject performing the action, so it is in the nominative case.

exercises.prompt: ___ іде до школи. (дівчинка — girl)
exercises.explanation: The subject of the sentence takes the nominative form: 'дівчинка'.

exercises.prompt: Which word is the subject (nominative)? 'Сонце світить яскраво.'
exercises.explanation: 'Сонце' (the sun) is the subject doing the shining — nominative case.

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Книгу лежить на столі.'
exercises.explanation: 'Книга' is the subject and must be in nominative ('книга'), not accusative ('книгу').

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order to make a sentence:
exercises.explanation: The subject 'Тато' (dad, nominative) comes first: 'Тато працює вдома.' (Dad works at home.)

exercises.prompt: Match each sentence with its nominative (subject) word:
exercises.explanation: The nominative word is always the subject — the one performing the action.

exercises.prompt: ___ дуже смачна. (вода — water)
exercises.explanation: 'Вода' is the subject and stays in its dictionary (nominative) form.

exercises.prompt: In 'Брат і сестра співають.', which words are in the nominative?
exercises.explanation: Both 'брат' and 'сестра' are subjects (nominative) — they are the ones singing.

exercises.prompt: Listen and type the subject of the sentence:
exercises.explanation: 'Бабуся' (grandmother) is the subject in nominative — she is cooking dinner.

exercises.prompt: Rewrite using 'вчитель' (teacher, m.) as the subject: '___ пояснює урок.'
exercises.explanation: The nominative form is simply 'вчитель' — no ending changes for the subject.

sections.title: Accusative: Inanimate Nouns
sections.explanation: The accusative marks the direct object — what receives the action. For inanimate masculine nouns and all neuter nouns, the accusative looks the same as nominative. Feminine nouns ending in -а change to -у, and -я to -ю.

exercises.prompt: Put 'книга' (book, f.) into the accusative case:
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -а change to -у in the accusative: книга → книгу.

exercises.prompt: Я купив ___. (хліб — bread, m. inanimate)
exercises.explanation: Inanimate masculine nouns keep their nominative form in the accusative: хліб stays хліб.

exercises.prompt: Which is the correct accusative? 'Вона п'є ___.' (кава — coffee, f.)
exercises.explanation: Feminine -а changes to -у in accusative: кава → каву.

exercises.prompt: Put 'пісня' (song, f.) into the accusative case:
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns ending in -я change to -ю in the accusative: пісня → пісню.

exercises.prompt: Match each noun with its accusative form:
exercises.explanation: Feminine -а→-у; inanimate masculine and neuter stay the same in accusative.

exercises.prompt: Ми дивимось ___. (фільм — film, m. inanimate)
exercises.explanation: 'Фільм' is inanimate masculine, so it stays the same in the accusative.

exercises.prompt: Which is the correct accusative? 'Він бачить ___.' (море — sea, n.)
exercises.explanation: Neuter nouns don't change in the accusative: море stays море.

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Я п'ю вода.'
exercises.explanation: 'Вода' is feminine and a direct object, so it must be in accusative: воду.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order:
exercises.explanation: 'Дідусь' (grandpa, nominative subject) reads 'газету' (newspaper, accusative object).

exercises.prompt: Listen and type the direct object (accusative word):
exercises.explanation: 'Сукню' (dress) is the direct object in accusative. Feminine: сукня → сукню (-я→-ю).

sections.title: Accusative: Animate Nouns
sections.explanation: Animate masculine nouns (people, animals) have a special accusative form that looks like the genitive: кіт→кота, брат→брата, друг→друга. Feminine animate nouns follow the same -а→-у rule.

exercises.prompt: Put 'кіт' (cat, m. animate) into the accusative case:
exercises.explanation: Animate masculine nouns take the genitive form in accusative: кіт → кота.

exercises.prompt: Я бачу ___. (брат — brother, m. animate)
exercises.explanation: Animate masculine accusative = genitive: брат → брата.

exercises.prompt: Which is the correct accusative? 'Вона любить ___.' (друг — friend, m. animate)
exercises.explanation: Animate masculine nouns take the genitive form in accusative: друг → друга.

exercises.prompt: Діти люблять ___. (собака — dog, f. animate)
exercises.hint: Feminine -а changes to -у even for animate nouns
exercises.explanation: Feminine animate nouns follow the same rule: -а→-у. Собака → собаку.

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Я знаю вчитель.'
exercises.explanation: 'Вчитель' (teacher) is animate masculine — accusative requires genitive form: вчителя.

exercises.prompt: Match each animate noun with its accusative form:
exercises.explanation: Animate masculine nouns take genitive endings; feminine nouns: -а→-у, -я→-ю.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order:
exercises.explanation: 'Дівчинка' (girl, nominative) sees 'кота' (cat, accusative animate).

exercises.prompt: In 'Ми чекаємо на лікаря.', why is it 'лікаря' and not 'лікар'?
options: Because 'лікар' is animate masculine, so accusative = genitive
Because it's in the dative case
exercises.explanation: 'Лікар' (doctor) is animate masculine, so the accusative form matches the genitive: лікаря.

exercises.prompt: Rewrite with 'студент' (student, m. animate) as the direct object: 'Професор бачить ___.'
exercises.explanation: Animate masculine accusative: студент → студента.

exercises.prompt: Listen and type the animate accusative word:
exercises.explanation: 'Сина' (son, accusative) — animate masculine: син → сина.

sections.title: Using Nominative & Accusative Together
sections.explanation: In a sentence, the subject is in nominative and the direct object is in accusative. Practice identifying and forming both cases in full sentences.
sections.tip: Subject (nominative) does the action → Object (accusative) receives it

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order (subject first, then verb, then object):
exercises.explanation: 'Учень' (student, nominative) writes 'лист' (letter, accusative — inanimate masculine, no change).

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Мама бачить кіт.'
exercises.explanation: 'Кіт' is animate masculine — the accusative form is 'кота'.

exercises.prompt: ___ варить ___. (мама — mom; каша — porridge)
exercises.hint: Subject in nominative, object in accusative (-а→-у)
exercises.explanation: 'Мама' (nominative subject) cooks 'кашу' (accusative of каша: -а→-у).

exercises.prompt: Change the object to accusative: 'Хлопець малює (картина — picture, f.).'
exercises.explanation: Feminine -а→-у in accusative: картина → картину.

exercises.prompt: In 'Тато купує машину.', identify the nominative and accusative words:
exercises.explanation: 'Тато' is the subject (nominative), 'машину' is the direct object (accusative of машина).

exercises.prompt: Match the sentence role to the correct case:
exercises.explanation: Nominative answers хто?/що? (who?/what?) for the subject. Accusative answers кого?/що? for the direct object.

exercises.prompt: Бабуся любить ___. (онук — grandson, m. animate)
exercises.explanation: Animate masculine accusative: онук → онука.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order:
exercises.explanation: 'Брат' (brother, nominative) waits for 'сестру' (sister, accusative: сестра→сестру).

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Собаку бачить хлопця.'
exercises.explanation: 'Собака' is the subject and should be in nominative (not 'собаку'). 'Хлопця' is the animate masculine accusative — correct.

exercises.prompt: Listen and type the full sentence, paying attention to nominative and accusative forms:
exercises.explanation: 'Вчитель' (teacher, nominative subject) checks 'зошит' (notebook, accusative — inanimate masculine, no change).
